9.1% ([[Nielsen Media Research#Nielsen TV ratings|Nielsen ratings]]){{cite news |url=https://www.tvb.org/wp-content/uploads/2021/10/Academy_Awards_Nielsen.pdf |title=Academy Awards Ratings |publisher=[[Television Bureau of Advertising]] |format=PDF |access-date=March 28, 2025 |archive-date=June 10, 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250610104012/https://www.tvb.org/wp-content/uploads/2021/10/Academy_Awards_Nielsen.pdf |url-status=live}} | last = 96th | next = 98th }} The '''97th Academy Awards''' ceremony, presented by the [[Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences]] (AMPAS), took place on March 2, 2025, at the [[Dolby Theatre]] in [[Hollywood, Los Angeles]]. During the gala, the AMPAS presented [[Academy Awards]] (commonly referred to as Oscars) in 23 categories honoring [[2024 in film|films released in 2024]]. The telecast drew 19.69 million viewers in the United States. ==Winners and nominees== The nominees for the 97th Academy Awards were announced on January 23, 2025, at the [[Samuel Goldwyn Theater]] in [[Beverly Hills, California|Beverly Hills]], by actress [[Rachel Sennott]] and actor [[Bowen Yang]].{{cite news |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/oscars-nominations-announcement-rachel-sennott-bowen-yang-1236113794/ |title=Oscars: Rachel Sennott and Bowen Yang to Announce Nominees |last=Nordyke |first=Kimberly |work=[[The Hollywood Reporter]] |date=January 22, 2025 |access-date=January 25, 2025 |archive-date=January 21, 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250121161752/https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/oscars-nominations-announcement-rachel-sennott-bowen-yang-1236113794/ |url-status=live}} ''[[Emilia Pérez]]'' led all nominees with thirteen nominations, the most for a non-English-language film in Oscars history; ''[[The Brutalist]]'' and ''[[Wicked (2024 film)|Wicked]]'' tied for second with ten nominations each.{{cite news |url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2025/jan/23/oscars-nominations-2025-emilia-perez-breaks-record-with-13-as-the-brutalist-and-wicked-both-trail-with-10 |title=Oscars nominations 2025: ''Emilia Pérez'' Breaks Record with 13 as ''The Brutalist'' and ''Wicked'' Both Trail with 10 |last=Shoard |first=Catherine |date=January 23, 2025 |work=[[The Guardian]] |access-date=January 23, 2025 |language=en-GB |archive-date=June 12, 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250612062452/http://web.archive.org/screenshot/https://www.theguardian.com/film/2025/jan/23/oscars-nominations-2025-emilia-perez-breaks-record-with-13-as-the-brutalist-and-wicked-both-trail-with-10 |url-status=live}} The winners were announced during the awards ceremony on March 2. Picture theatrical qualification and other rule changes=== In June 2023, the Academy announced that, starting with the 97th awards, all films submitted for Best Picture would have to comply with several standards regarding their theatrical runs.{{cite magazine |last=Grein |first=Paul |title=Oscars Set New Theatrical Standards for Best Picture Eligibility |url=https://www.billboard.com/music/awards/oscars-new-theatrical-standards-best-picture-eligibility-1235359390/ |access-date=March 28, 2025 |magazine=[[Billboard (magazine)|Billboard]] |date=June 21, 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230702181701/https://www.billboard.com/music/awards/oscars-new-theatrical-standards-best-picture-eligibility-1235359390/ |archive-date=July 2, 2023 |language=en-US |url-status=live}} In a press release, AMPAS president [[Janet Yang]] and Academy CEO [[Bill Kramer (executive)|Bill Kramer]] said of the new standards, "In support of our mission to celebrate and honor the arts and sciences of moviemaking, it is our hope that this expanded theatrical footprint will increase the visibility of films worldwide and encourage audiences to experience our artform in a theatrical setting. Based on many conversations with industry partners, we feel that this evolution benefits film artists and movie lovers alike."{{cite news |last=Pond |first=Steve |title=Oscars Toughen Theatrical Requirements to Qualify for Best Picture |url=https://www.thewrap.com/oscars-toughen-theatrical-requirements-to-qualify-for-best-picture/ |access-date=April 11, 2025 |work=[[TheWrap]] |date=June 21, 2023 |archive-date=March 20, 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250320125459/https://www.thewrap.com/oscars-toughen-theatrical-requirements-to-qualify-for-best-picture/ |url-status=live}} Many media outlets pointed out that the new rules were drafted in response to the growing presence of streaming platforms such as [[Amazon Prime Video]], [[Apple TV (streaming service)|Apple TV+]], and [[Netflix]] submitting their films for awards consideration.Multiple sources: * {{cite news |last1=Rottenberg |first1=Josh |last2=Whipp |first2=Glenn |title=The Oscars Are Changing the Rules for Best Picture. Here's What It Could Mean |url=https://www.latimes.com/entertainment-arts/awards/story/2023-06-21/oscars-best-picture-academy-awards-eligibility-rules-changes |url-access=subscription |access-date=March 28, 2025 |work=[[Los Angeles Times]] |date=June 21, 2023 |archive-date=January 5, 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250105151654/https://www.latimes.com/entertainment-arts/awards/story/2023-06-21/oscars-best-picture-academy-awards-eligibility-rules-changes |url-status=live}} * {{cite news |last=Sperling |first=Nicole |title=Oscars' Best Picture Hopefuls Must Spend More Time in Theaters |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2023/06/21/business/media/academy-awards-best-picture-eligibility.html |url-access=subscription |access-date=March 28, 2024 |newspaper=[[The New York Times]] |date=June 21, 2023 |archive-date=January 17, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240117054152/https://www.nytimes.com/2023/06/21/business/media/academy-awards-best-picture-eligibility.html |url-status=live}} * {{cite news |last=Feinberg |first=Scott |title=Oscars: Film Academy Lengthens Minimum Theatrical Release Required for Best Picture Eligibility |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/oscars-theatrical-release-best-picture-eligibility-1235521130/ |access-date=March 28, 2024 |work=[[The Hollywood Reporter]] |date=June 21, 2023 |archive-date=May 1, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240501023823/https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/oscars-theatrical-release-best-picture-eligibility-1235521130/ |url-status=live}} * {{cite news |last=Davis |first=Clayton |title=Oscars to Require 'Expanded Theatrical Run' to Qualify for Best Picture Beginning in 2025 |url=https://variety.com/2023/film/awards/oscars-theatrical-expansion-requirements-best-picture-1235651334/ |access-date=March 28, 2024 |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=June 21, 2023 |archive-date=April 22, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240422190917/https://variety.com/2023/film/awards/oscars-theatrical-expansion-requirements-best-picture-1235651334/ |url-status=live}} According to the new rules, in addition to the previous requirement where prospective films must play theatrically for a week in either Los Angeles, New York City, Chicago, the San Francisco Bay Area, Atlanta, or Miami,{{efn|In April 2024, the Academy replaced Miami with Dallas as one of six cities where a film may debut to qualify for awards consideration by the Academy.{{cite news |last=Pedersen |first=Erik |title=New Rules for 2025 Oscars Affect Best Picture, Drive-Ins, Qualifying Metro Areas & More |url=https://deadline.com/2024/04/oscars-rule-changes-best-picture-drive-ins-1235891653/ |access-date=March 28, 2025 |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |date=April 22, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20241111203627/https://deadline.com/2024/04/oscars-rule-changes-best-picture-drive-ins-1235891653/ |archive-date=November 11, 2024 |language=en-US |url-status=live}}}} they also would have to be shown in an expanded seven-day theatrical run in 10 of the top 50 US markets. The expanded theatrical run would have to occur within 45 days after the film's initial release in the eligibility year, and can be shown in either consecutive or nonconsecutive days. For films released later in the calendar year with expansions after January 10, distributors would have to submit to AMPAS release plans for verification and the theatrical run would have to be completed no later than January 24. Non-American films could also have theatrical runs in two markets outside the US to be counted toward ten theatrical markets needed for consideration. However, these films would have to release in the top 15 international theatrical markets in addition to the film's country of origin.{{cite news |last=Hammond |first=Pete |title=Oscars: Academy Approves Major Change to Best Picture Eligibility Rules Requiring More Extensive Theatrical Runs |url=https://deadline.com/2023/06/oscars-academ-major-change-best-picture-eligibility-rules-1235314790/ |access-date=September 14, 2023 |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |date=June 21, 2023 |archive-date=August 12, 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230812053151/https://deadline.com/2023/06/oscars-academ-major-change-best-picture-eligibility-rules-1235314790/ |url-status=live}} Additional rule changes were also approved by the AMPAS Board of Governors in April 2024. [[Drive-in theater]]s, which were temporarily allowed as a venue to meet qualification and eligibility standards during the [[COVID-19 pandemic]], would be no longer recognized as a method to fulfill requirements for consideration.{{cite news |last=Feinberg |first=Scott |title=Oscars: Film Academy Updates Rules and Campaign Protocols, Announces Changes to Special Awards |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/oscars-2025-changes-rules-special-awards-campaigns-1235878854/ |access-date=July 15, 2025 |work=[[The Hollywood Reporter]] |date=April 22, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250312085956/https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/oscars-2025-changes-rules-special-awards-campaigns-1235878854/ |archive-date=March 12, 2025 |language=en-US |url-status=live}} In the Best Original Score category, up to three composers can receive individual statuettes if all composers contribute significantly to a film's musical score. Additionally, the shortlist for the aforementioned category would increase from fifteen to twenty films. For the screenplay awards, all films must submit a final shooting script to be eligible for consideration in either category. Animated feature films submitted for consideration in the Best International Feature Film category may also be eligible for the Best Animated Feature award provided they meet the rules outlined for both categories. Finally, recipients of the [[Irving G. Thalberg Memorial Award]] will now receive an Oscar statuette as opposed to a bust of Thalberg in previous years.{{cite news |last=Davis |first=Clayton |title=Oscar Rules Updated for 2025 Awards: Original Score Shortlist Increased, Drive-In Theater Eligibility Removed and More |url=https://variety.com/2024/film/awards/oscars-rules-2025-original-score-drive-in-movies-1235978114/ |access-date=March 28, 2025 |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=April 22, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240828000826/https://variety.com/2024/film/awards/oscars-rules-2025-original-score-drive-in-movies-1235978114/ |archive-date=August 28, 2024 |language=en-US |url-status=live}} ===Box office performance of Best Picture nominees=== When the nominations were announced, nine of the ten films nominated for Best Picture had earned a combined gross of $900 million at the American and Canadian box offices at the time. ''Wicked'' was the highest-grossing film among the Best Picture nominees with $466 million in domestic box office receipts. ''Dune: Part Two'' came in second with $283 million; this was followed by ''A Complete Unknown'' ($59 million), ''Conclave'' ($32 million), ''The Substance'' ($17 million), ''Anora'' ($15 million), ''The Brutalist'' ($6 million), ''Nickel Boys'' ($1.1 million), and ''I'm Still Here'' ($162,000).{{cite news |title=A Look at How the Oscar Best Picture Nominees Have Fared at the Box Office |url=https://apnews.com/article/oscar-best-picture-nominees-2025-box-office-dc15532ebaf9544321d01cf04272fcdb |access-date=March 28, 2025 |publisher=[[Associated Press]] |date=January 23, 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250126191645/https://apnews.com/article/oscar-best-picture-nominees-2025-box-office-dc15532ebaf9544321d01cf04272fcdb |archive-date=January 26, 2025 |language=en-US |url-status=live}} The box office figures for ''Emilia Pérez'' were unavailable due to distributor Netflix's policy of refusing to release such figures.{{cite news |last=Coyle |first=Jake |title=The ''Emilia Pérez'' Backlash, Explained |url=https://apnews.com/article/emilia-perez-oscars-backlash-eb75830df1d413779daacc331cd2b8e6 |access-date=February 13, 2025 |publisher=[[Associated Press]] |date=January 31, 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250411192548/https://apnews.com/article/emilia-perez-oscars-backlash-eb75830df1d413779daacc331cd2b8e6 |archive-date=April 11, 2025 |url-status=live}} ===Critical reviews=== ''[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]]'' chief film critic [[Owen Gleiberman]] called the show "a brisk and elegantly executed piece of media stagecraft, successful in more ways than not. It was lavishly designed and purged of dead spots, and the winners were given the chance to reveal who they were, which is really the heart of the show."{{cite news |last=Gleiberman |first=Owen |author-link=Owen Gleiberman |title=Oscars Review: Conan O'Brien Rocked His Hosting Debut, but the Telecast Felt Too Small for Hollywood's Biggest Night |url=https://variety.com/2025/tv/reviews/oscars-review-conan-o-brien-anora-timothee-chalament-1236324874/ |access-date=March 28, 2025 |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=March 3, 2025 |archive-date=March 3, 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250303190630/https://variety.com/2025/tv/reviews/oscars-review-conan-o-brien-anora-timothee-chalament-1236324874/ |url-status=live}} Columnist Brian Lowry of ''[[TheWrap]]'' commented, "The 97th Academy Awards largely mastered the delicate mechanics of that juggling act, acknowledging weightier matters — such as bringing out Los Angeles firefighters for an extended ovation — while seeking to provide a sense of playfulness, even silliness, courtesy of host Conan O'Brien."{{cite news |last=Lowry |first=Brian |title=Oscars Review: Conan O'Brien and Company Find the Right Delicate Balance in a Chaotic Climate |url=https://www.thewrap.com/oscars-review-conan-obrien-host/ |access-date=March 11, 2025 |work=[[TheWrap]] |date=March 2, 2025 |archive-date=March 12, 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250312000052/https://www.thewrap.com/oscars-review-conan-obrien-host/ |url-status=live}} Television critic Judy Berman of ''[[Time (magazine)|Time]]'' praised O'Brien's hosting performance, writing, "Whenever O'Brien was onstage, balancing impish humor with a palpable love for movies and compassion for an industry where so many recently lost so much to the wildfires that ravaged Los Angeles, the ceremony felt light and loose." She concluded her remarks by stating, "Still, the best moments of this hosting gig were just Conan being Conan, letting the winners enjoy their spotlight and laughing at himself to make everyone else feel comfortable."{{cite magazine |last=Berman |first=Judy |title=Conan Nailed the Oscars Hosting Job Simply by Being Conan |url=https://time.com/7263695/oscars-2025-review-conan-obrien/ |access-date=March 11, 2025 |magazine=[[Time (magazine)|Time]] |date=March 2, 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250611071934/https://time.com/7263695/oscars-2025-review-conan-obrien/ |archive-date=June 11, 2025 |language=en-US |url-status=live}} Kelly Lawler of ''[[USA Today]]'' reserved praise for O'Brien and the winners' speeches, but she wrote, "Inoffensive and somewhat bland, the 2025 Oscars lacked passion and fervor, or a distinctive hook that makes the ceremony more than just a generic recitation of winners."{{cite news |last=Lawler |first=Kelly |title=The 2025 Oscars Were Perfectly Pleasant in Every Way. But Where Was the Passion? |url=https://www.usatoday.com/story/entertainment/movies/2025/03/03/2025-oscars-review/80524355007/ |access-date=March 28, 2025 |work=[[USA Today]] |date=March 3, 2025 |archive-date=June 10, 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250610115751/https://www.usatoday.com/story/entertainment/movies/2025/03/03/2025-oscars-review/80524355007/ |url-status=live}} Television critic Daniel Fienberg of ''[[The Hollywood Reporter]]'' remarked, "I'm perfectly happy to pretend that this telecast was the show we deserved at this point in history, when everything feels so unstable. But maybe it was just an uneven mess. Who's to say?" He criticized several decisions such as dropping the performances of the Best Original Song nominees in favor of musical tributes to James Bond and Quincy Jones that contributed to the telecast's bloated runtime, but he commended O'Brien's hosting performance and called writing during the gala "impressively sharp".{{cite news |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/tv/tv-reviews/oscars-2025-review-conan-o-brien-adrien-brody-karla-sofia-gascon-1236152904/ |title=Critic's Notebook: Conan O'Brien Helps to Steady an Erratic Oscars Telecast |last=Fienberg |first=Daniel |work=[[The Hollywood Reporter]] |date=March 2, 2025 |access-date=March 3, 2025 |archive-date=April 29, 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250429011535/https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/tv/tv-reviews/oscars-2025-review-conan-o-brien-adrien-brody-karla-sofia-gascon-1236152904/ |url-status=live}} ''[[The Detroit News]]'' film critic Adam Graham commented, "Sunday night's Academy Awards telecast was severely lacking in the movie star razzle dazzle we look forward to on the broadcast. Coming at the end of a packed awards and entertainment season, it felt more like reheated leftovers than it did Hollywood's biggest night."{{cite news |last=Graham |first=Adam |title=''Anora'' Triumphs at Starpower-Starved Oscars |url=https://www.detroitnews.com/story/entertainment/movies/2025/03/03/anora-triumphs-at-starpower-starved-oscars/81111572007/ |url-access=subscription |access-date=March 11, 2025 |newspaper=[[The Detroit News]] |date=March 3, 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250612061955/https://www.detroitnews.com/story/entertainment/movies/2025/03/03/anora-triumphs-at-starpower-starved-oscars/81111572007/ |archive-date=June 12, 2025 |language=en-US |url-status=live}} ===Ratings and reception=== The American telecast on ABC drew in an average of 19.69 million people over its length, which was a 1% increase from the previous year's ceremony.{{cite news |last=Campione |first=Katie |date=March 4, 2025 |title=Oscars Audience Up to 19.69 Million After Disney Adds Mobile & PC Viewing |url=https://deadline.com/2025/03/oscar-ratings-2025-1236309304/ |access-date=March 28, 2025 |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250328091511/https://deadline.com/2025/03/oscar-ratings-2025-1236309304/ |archive-date=March 28, 2025 |language=en-US |url-status=live}} The show also had 9.1% of households watching the ceremony based on [[Nielsen Media Research|Nielsen]] estimates, which was lower than the previous ceremony. In addition, it garnered a higher 18–49 demo rating with a 4.54 rating among viewers in that demographic.{{cite news |url=https://variety.com/2025/tv/news/oscars-ratings-2025-viewers-1236326364/
